children.name: Gracilinanus
children.description: Gracilinanus — a genus of opossums in the family Didelphidae.

children.name: Gracilinanus aceramarcae
children.description: The Aceramarca gracile opossum or Bolivian gracile opossum is a species of opossum. It is native to Bolivia and Peru, where it occurs in tropical elfin forest habitat.

children.name: Gracilinanus agilis
children.description: The agile gracile opossum is an opossum species from South America. It is found in Argentina, Bolivia, Brazil, Colombia, Peru, Paraguay, and Uruguay.

children.name: Gracilinanus agricolai
children.description: Agricola's gracile opossum is a species of opossum in the family Didelphidae endemic to eastern Brazil. Its habitat is the caatinga and cerrado. While its conservation status has not been determined, expansion of agricultural activities is leading to loss of some of its habitat.

children.name: Gracilinanus dryas
children.description: The wood sprite gracile opossum is a mammal. It is a species of opossum in the family Didelphidae. It is found in Colombia and Venezuela.

children.name: Gracilinanus emiliae
children.description: Emilia's gracile opossum is an opossum species from South America. It is found in Brazil, Colombia, French Guiana, and Suriname.

children.name: Gracilinanus formosus
children.description: The Chacoan pygmy opossum is a recently described genus and species of didelphimorph marsupial. The only species in Chacodelphys, C. formosa, was known until 2004 from only one specimen collected in 1920 in the Chaco of Formosa Province, Argentina.

children.name: Gracilinanus ignitus
children.description: The red-bellied gracile opossum is an extinct species of opossum that was native to Jujuy Province in northwest Argentina. Its forest habitat has been destroyed, and it was last seen in 1962. It is likely a synonym of the Chacoan gracile opossum.

children.name: Gracilinanus marica
children.description: The northern gracile opossum is a species of opossum in the family Didelphidae. It is found in Colombia and Venezuela. marica naturally occurs in subtropical or tropical moist lowland forests.

children.name: Gracilinanus microtarsus
children.description: The Brazilian gracile opossum is a species of small opossum from Brazil.

children.name: Marmosa macrotarsus
children.description: The Quechuan mouse opossum, also called the western Amazonian mouse opossum, is a South American opossum species of the family Didelphidae. It was known from only two areas of montane forest on the eastern slopes of the Andes in Peru, at altitudes from 300 to 2700 m.: at the type locality in the Valle de Occobamba is in the southern area, in Cuzco Region, and in the northern area is in the vicinity of Moyobamba in San Martín Region. A 2014 survey found this species in Bolivia and Brazil.

children.name: Marmosa tyleriana
children.description: Tyler's mouse opossum is a South American marsupial of the family Didelphidae. It lives in rainforests of the Guiana Highlands of southern Venezuela at elevations between 1300 and 2200 m. The species has only been found on three isolated tepuis.
